channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New Xbox Boot Up Sound Revealed By CEO
item.content:encoded:
Xbox CEO has revealed a new boot-up sound for consoles, which should arrive next week.

Since the arrival of the new CEO, we have seen all kinds of changes at Xbox, including reducing the price of Game Pass, revealing new tiers, and much more. The latest change has been made to match the brand-new Xbox logo; however, the new sound feels awfully similar to the previous iteration.

New Xbox Boot-Up Sound Makes Small Tweaks Over the Previous One

Interested readers can check out the new Xbox boot-up sound in the CEO Asha Sharma’s latest post on X (formerly Twitter). Sharma also stated that the new sound will arrive next Wednesday, May 13. Though the latest version and the previous iteration of the sound are very close, there are some audible tweaks. The sound feels higher-pitched, is shorter in duration, and matches the animation they have set for the new logo pretty well. Interested readers can check out the comparison video below:

Since Asha Sharma’s arrival, players have been seeing more frequent changes towards Xbox. Just recently, the CEO announced that Microsoft will no longer develop Copilot for Xbox and is winding down mobile development as well. Along with that, the Xbox leadership is seeing further changes: more former Microsoft CoreAI folks have joined the company’s leadership team.

In other news, Xbox Game Pass brings a stacked lineup for May 2026 Wave 1 with Forza Horizon 6 headlining the collection. Here are more details. Microsoft has announced a new Game Dev Update show that will give an update on Xbox’s Project Helix next-gen console. What are your thoughts on the new Xbox boot-up sound? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 director being open to exploring DLC possibilities. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Director Open to Explore DLC Possibilities
item.content:encoded:
The director of the Final Fantasy 7 Remake series has stated that he isn’t against the idea of DLC for Part 3.

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 is a game that many players have been waiting for, especially since the first two installments appeared on Nintendo and Xbox platforms. It seems that fans might be more excited about this entry as a DLC hasn’t been ruled out.

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 DLC is a Possibility If There’s Enough Interest

During a new interview with ntower (via GamesRadar+), Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 director Naoki Hamaguchi discussed DLC possibilities. Hamaguchi first explained why they didn’t release a DLC for the previous game, stating that they had “considered the possibility of developing a story DLC right up until immediately before the project officially moved into the planning phase.”

They ended up not going ahead with the idea, as they wanted to work on the third installment in parallel, to help with the release plans and quality of the third game. The main goal of the devs was “to deliver the third entry to fans as quickly as possible at a level of polish they would find satisfying.”

At the same time, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 could get DLC, as Naoki Hamaguchi stated that it depends on “how strongly fans continue to support Final Fantasy 7 as both a title and a franchise.” If the demand is enough, the team is “open to exploring those possibilities proactively.”

In other news, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 will feature new gameplay mechanics, and the director has already completed over 40 full playthroughs of the upcoming title. What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 DLC not being ruled out? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Nintendo being pressured to raise Switch 2 prices. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
